gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A constructed data value: a saturated constructor application (RFC-0011 §4.1, W6) with a"] # [doc = " meet-summary guarantee."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Recursion-safety (RFC-0041 §4.5, W3):** [`Clone`], [`PartialEq`], and [`Drop`] are **manual,"] # [doc = " iterative** (below), *not* `#[derive]`d, and the content hash ([`Canon::datum`]) is iterative —"] # [doc = " a derived (recursive) form overflows the native stack (`SIGABRT`, violating never-silent G2) on a"] # [doc = " deeply-nested `Datum`↔[`CoreValue`] chain (e.g. a `S(S(S(…Z)))` numeral). All four are"] # [doc = " bit-identical to the derived forms (mutation-witnessed; §6 within-freeze hardening bar (a))."] # [derive (Debug)] pub struct Datum { ctor : CtorRef , fields : Vec < CoreValue > , guarantee : GuaranteeStrength , }

gaps.snippet: impl Datum { # [doc = " Construct a datum from a constructor reference and its field values, computing the"] # [doc = " meet-summary guarantee = `meet` of the fields' guarantees with the intrinsic `Exact`"] # [doc = " (construction adds no approximation — RFC-0011 §4.6). With all-`Exact` fields the summary is"] # [doc = " `Exact`, consistent with M-I1 (no bound)."] # [must_use] pub fn new (ctor : CtorRef , fields : Vec < CoreValue >) -> Self { let guarantee = GuaranteeStrength :: meet_all (fields . iter () . map (CoreValue :: guarantee)) ; Datum { ctor , fields , guarantee , } } # [doc = " The constructor reference (`#T#i`)."] # [must_use] pub fn ctor (& self) -> & CtorRef { & self . ctor } # [doc = " The field values, in declaration order."] # [must_use] pub fn fields (& self) -> & [CoreValue] { & self . fields } # [doc = " The meet-summary guarantee."] # [must_use] pub fn guarantee (& self) -> GuaranteeStrength { self . guarantee } # [doc = " This datum with its summary guarantee met against `g` (weakest-wins). Used by `Match` to"] # [doc = " fold the scrutinee's guarantee into a data result (RFC-0011 §4.6); never upgrades (VR-5)."] # [must_use] pub fn meet_guarantee (mut self , g : GuaranteeStrength) -> Self { self . guarantee = self . guarantee . meet (g) ; self } # [doc = " The identity-bearing content hash of this datum: its constructor reference and its fields'"] # [doc = " content (the guarantee summary is dynamic metadata — excluded, like `Meta` on a [`Value`];"] # [doc = " RFC-0001 §4.6)."] # [must_use] pub fn content_hash (& self) -> ContentHash { let mut c = Canon :: new () ; c . datum (self) ; c . finish () } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Iterative deep clone of a [`CoreValue`] and the `Datum`s it contains, bit-identical to the"] # [doc = " derived clone (the stored datum `guarantee` summary is preserved verbatim — **not** recomputed"] # [doc = " via [`Datum::new`], which `meet`s and could differ). Expand/assemble worklist over a `CoreValue`"] # [doc = " value stack (`done.pop()` yields fields in forward declaration order)."] fn clone_core (root : & CoreValue) -> CoreValue { enum Task < 'a > { Expand (& 'a CoreValue) , AssembleData { ctor : CtorRef , guarantee : GuaranteeStrength , arity : usize , } , } let mut tasks : Vec < Task < '_ > > = vec ! [Task :: Expand (root)] ; let mut done : Vec < CoreValue > = Vec :: new () ; while let Some (task) = tasks . pop () { match task { Task :: Expand (cv) => match cv { CoreValue :: Repr (v) => done . push (CoreValue :: Repr (v . clone ())) , CoreValue :: Data (d) => { tasks . push (Task :: AssembleData { ctor : d . ctor . clone () , guarantee : d . guarantee , arity : d . fields . len () , }) ; for f in & d . fields { tasks . push (Task :: Expand (f)) ; } } } , Task :: AssembleData { ctor , guarantee , arity , } => { let mut fields = Vec :: with_capacity (arity) ; for _ in 0 .. arity { fields . push (done . pop () . expect ("clone_core: datum field")) ; } done . push (CoreValue :: Data (Datum { ctor , fields , guarantee , })) ; } } } done . pop () . expect ("clone_core: exactly one root value remains") }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Iterative structural equality of two [`CoreValue`]s (and their nested `Datum`s), result-identical"] # [doc = " to the derived `PartialEq` (which for a `Datum` compares `ctor`, `fields`, **and** the"] # [doc = " `guarantee` summary — all reproduced here). Bounded native stack regardless of nesting depth."] fn eq_core (a : & CoreValue , b : & CoreValue) -> bool { let mut stack : Vec < (& CoreValue , & CoreValue) > = vec ! [(a , b)] ; while let Some ((x , y)) = stack . pop () { match (x , y) { (CoreValue :: Repr (v1) , CoreValue :: Repr (v2)) => { if v1 != v2 { return false ; } } (CoreValue :: Data (d1) , CoreValue :: Data (d2)) => { if d1 . ctor != d2 . ctor || d1 . guarantee != d2 . guarantee || d1 . fields . len () != d2 . fields . len () { return false ; } for (p , q) in d1 . fields . iter () . zip (& d2 . fields) { stack . push ((p , q)) ; } } _ => return false , } } true }

gaps.snippet: impl Canon { # [doc = " Encode a [`CoreValue`]'s identity-bearing content (a representation value's repr+payload, or"] # [doc = " a datum's constructor+fields). `Meta` / the datum summary are dynamic and excluded."] # [doc = " Iterative for the `Data` arm (delegates to the iterative [`Canon::datum`])."] pub (crate) fn core_value (& mut self , v : & CoreValue) { match v { CoreValue :: Repr (rv) => self . value (rv) , CoreValue :: Data (d) => self . datum (d) , } } # [doc = " Encode a [`Datum`]: its constructor reference then each field (order significant), **pre-order"] # [doc = " and iterative** (RFC-0041 §4.5) — byte-for-byte identical to the former recursive encoding, so"] # [doc = " content addresses are unchanged, but with bounded native-stack use on a deep datum spine."] pub (crate) fn datum (& mut self , d : & Datum) { self . datum_header (d) ; let mut work : Vec < & CoreValue > = Vec :: new () ; for f in d . fields () . iter () . rev () { work . push (f) ; } while let Some (cv) = work . pop () { match cv { CoreValue :: Repr (rv) => self . value (rv) , CoreValue :: Data (inner) => { self . datum_header (inner) ; for f in inner . fields () . iter () . rev () { work . push (f) ; } } } } } # [doc = " Emit one datum's header bytes (tag · ctor-ref · field count) — the per-node prefix the"] # [doc = " recursive `datum` encoder wrote before recursing into fields."] fn datum_header (& mut self , d : & Datum) { self . tag (crate :: content :: tag :: DATUM) ; self . ctor_ref (d . ctor ()) ; self . u64 (d . fields () . len () as u64) ; } }
